command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 1 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> {{ Add example code here }}

dev:remarks.maml:para: {{ Add example description here }}

maml:description.maml:para: Add lab disk definition.
Adds a disk definition to the current lab. Disk definitions can be used by lab machines.
The disk name.
The disk size in GB.
The label to assign.
The drive letter to assign.
The allocation unit size in Bytes to use, e.g. 64kb.
MBR, GPT
Indicates that the disk object should be passed back to the caller.
Indicates that the initialization of the disk with a file system should be skipped.
Indicates that large FRS should be used.
The allocation unit size in Bytes to use, e.g. 64kb.
The disk size in GB.
The drive letter to assign.
The label to assign.
The disk name.
MBR, GPT
Indicates that the disk object should be passed back to the caller.
Indicates that the initialization of the disk with a file system should be skipped.
Indicates that large FRS should be used.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 1 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> Add-LabDiskDefinition -Name SQL_DataDrive -DiskSizeInGb 100 -Label Data -DriveLetter D -AllocationUnitSize 64kb PS C:\> Add-LabMachineDefinition -Name SQL01 -Disk SQL_DataDrive

dev:remarks.maml:para: Creates a new disk definition and attaches the disk to the machine definition SQL01.

maml:description.maml:para: Add a definition of an Active Directory domain or forest to the lab

Adds a definition of an Active Directory domain or forest name together with username which will be the administrator of the domain as well as the password. This information is used for AutomatedLab to be able to logon to all machines during and after deployment using domain credentials when needed.
Name of Active Directory domain or forest in FQDN format.
Desired username of the administrator when deploying a new lab or existing user of the administrator if adding to a lab or an existing external (not deployed by AutomatedLab) domain.
Desired password of the administrator when deploying a new lab or password of an existing administrator if adding to a lab or an existing external (not deployed by AutomatedLab) domain.
Whether or not to return the actual created domain definition in the call of this function to enable you to continue working on a pipeline
Desired password of the administrator when deploying a new lab or password of an existing administrator if adding to a lab or an existing external (not deployed by AutomatedLab) domain.
Desired username of the administrator when deploying a new lab or existing user of the administrator if adding to a lab or an existing external (not deployed by AutomatedLab) domain.
Name of Active Directory domain or forest in FQDN format.
Whether or not to return the actual created domain definition in the call of this function to enable you to continue working on a pipeline

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 1 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> Add-LabDomainDefinition -Name comtoso.com -AdminUser Install -AdminPassword Somepass1 PS C:\> Set-LabInstallationCredential -User Install -Password Somepass1

dev:remarks.maml:para: Configures the contoso domain for the entire lab. Take care to select the same installation credentials, otherwise the domain controller deployment will not work.

maml:description.maml:para: Adds a definition of an ISO file using a logical name and a path of the ISO file

When AutomatedLab is to install roles like SQL Server, Exchange, Visual Studio, Office etc, it must know where to find the ISO file for these products. AutomatedLab does this by associating a logical name with a definition of an ISO file by path. This way, when AutomatedLab needs an ISO file, it looks for this "mapping".

All operating system ISO files are detected automatically UNLESS the ISO files are not placed within the LabSources folder structure. If so, you must specify manually using Add-LabIsoImageDefinition, where the ISO file is located.
Logical name for reference. Names are case sensitive.
In order to deploy certain roles like SQL, TFS, ... use the role name for the ISO, e.g. SQLServer2016
Path of ISO file
Indicates that the ISO is an OS installation disk
Indicates that no output should be visible
Indicates that the ISO is an OS installation disk
Logical name for reference. Names are case sensitive.
In order to deploy certain roles like SQL, TFS, ... use the role name for the ISO, e.g. SQLServer2016
Indicates that no output should be visible
Path of ISO file

dev:remarks.maml:para: Adds a definition of ISO file to be used when installing Server 2016.
Adds a definition of ISO file to be used when installing SQL Server 2014.

maml:description.maml:para: Adds a definition of a machine to the lab
Adds a definition of a machine to the lab. This does not create the actual machine. It merely creates the information of how the machines should look like.
Attempt to activate the Windows VM online or using KMS
The domain for the auto logon user
The password for the auto logon user
The user name for the auto logon user

The Azure properties. Don't add them unless you know what you are doing. Currently valid properties: 'ResourceGroupName', 'UseAllRoleSizes', 'RoleSize', 'LoadBalancerRdpPort', 'LoadBalancerWinRmHttpPort', 'LoadBalancerWinRmHttpsPort', 'SubnetName','UseByolImage'
The role size of the machine on Azure
The default domain for the machine
The disk names created by Add-LabDiskDefinition
The first DNS server for the machine
The second DNS server for the machine
The domain name of the machine.
Indicates that Windows firewall should be enabled on the machine
The default gateway for the machine
The HyperV properties. Currently valid properties: - AutomaticStartAction, default Nothing, refer to Set-VM
- AutomaticStartDelay, default 0, refer to Set-VM
- AutomaticStopAction, default ShutDown, refer to Set-VM
- EnableTpm, if value is 1, true or yes, TPM will be enabled
- EnableSecureBoot, if value is on, SecureBoot will be enabled
- SecureBootTemplate, MicrosoftWindows or MicrosoftUEFICertificateAuthority
Path to an initial mof (machine configuration) file. Applied at first boot.
Path to an initial meta.mof (LCM configuration) file. Applied at first boot.
The credentials of the installation user (i.e. the local admin)
The static private IP address of this machine
Indicates that the machine should be joined to a lab domain
If using AD-based KMS, specify the domain to look up the KMS key
If specifying a standalone KMS, specify its port
Name of a standalone KMS Server
Maximum dynamic memory for machine Specified in bytes and must be within range 128MB-128GB.
Startup memory for machine or, if -MinMemory or -MaxMemory is not specified, static memory for machine. Specified in bytes and must be within range 128MB-128GB.
Minimum dynamic memory for machine Specified in bytes and must be within range 128MB-128GB.
Name of machine Name must consist of characters a-z, A-Z, '-' or 0-9 and must be 1-15 in length.
The lab network to connect this machine to
Notes to add to the machine
The lab operating system to use
The operating system version
The organizational unit to join this VM into
Indicates that the machine definition should be passed back to the caller
Post installation activities as defined with Get-LabPostInstallationActivity
List of activities to run before the machine is installed
Virtual processor count for machine
Path to a difference Reference Disk to be used for this machine
Name of the resource in the resource group
The RHEL packages to install
The machine's role definitions. See `[enum]::GetValues([AutomatedLab.Roles])` or <https://automatedlab.org/en/latest/Wiki/Roles/roles/> for more information.
Indicates that this machine is already deployed somewhere and should only be included in the lab. When the lab is removed, these machines are not destroyed.
The path to your SSH private key used to connect to this VM. Only supported on PowerShell 6 and newer
The path to your SSH public key used to connect to this VM. Only supported on PowerShell 6 and newer.
The list of SUSE packages to install during AutoYast stage
The machine's time zone
The local tools path to be copied to the machine
The tools path on the destination machine
The list of Ubuntu packages to install during cloudinit stage
The locale to use
The virtualization host to use. It is recommended to define the default virtualization host when creating a new lab
Specify VM generation on Azure and Hyper-V. Only valid if supported, possible values are 1 and 2
Attempt to activate the Windows VM online or using KMS
The domain for the auto logon user
The password for the auto logon user
The user name for the auto logon user

The Azure properties. Don't add them unless you know what you are doing. Currently valid properties: 'ResourceGroupName', 'UseAllRoleSizes', 'RoleSize', 'LoadBalancerRdpPort', 'LoadBalancerWinRmHttpPort', 'LoadBalancerWinRmHttpsPort', 'SubnetName','UseByolImage'
The role size of the machine on Azure
The default domain for the machine
The disk names created by Add-LabDiskDefinition
The domain name of the machine.
Indicates that Windows firewall should be enabled on the machine
The HyperV properties. Currently valid properties: - AutomaticStartAction, default Nothing, refer to Set-VM
- AutomaticStartDelay, default 0, refer to Set-VM
- AutomaticStopAction, default ShutDown, refer to Set-VM
- EnableTpm, if value is 1, true or yes, TPM will be enabled
- EnableSecureBoot, if value is on, SecureBoot will be enabled
- SecureBootTemplate, MicrosoftWindows or MicrosoftUEFICertificateAuthority
Path to an initial mof (machine configuration) file. Applied at first boot.
Path to an initial meta.mof (LCM configuration) file. Applied at first boot.
The credentials of the installation user (i.e. the local admin)
Indicates that the machine should be joined to a lab domain
If using AD-based KMS, specify the domain to look up the KMS key
If specifying a standalone KMS, specify its port
Name of a standalone KMS Server
Maximum dynamic memory for machine Specified in bytes and must be within range 128MB-128GB.
Startup memory for machine or, if -MinMemory or -MaxMemory is not specified, static memory for machine. Specified in bytes and must be within range 128MB-128GB.
Minimum dynamic memory for machine Specified in bytes and must be within range 128MB-128GB.
Name of machine Name must consist of characters a-z, A-Z, '-' or 0-9 and must be 1-15 in length.
The lab network adapter to connect this machine to
Notes to add to the machine
The lab operating system to use
The operating system version
The organizational unit to join this VM into
Indicates that the machine definition should be passed back to the caller
Post installation activities as defined with Get-LabPostInstallationActivity
List of activities to run before the machine is installed
Virtual processor count for machine
Path to a difference Reference Disk to be used for this machine
Name of the resource in the resource group
The RHEL packages to install
The machine's role definitions. See `[enum]::GetValues([AutomatedLab.Roles])` or <https://automatedlab.org/en/latest/Wiki/Roles/roles/> for more information.
Indicates that this machine is already deployed somewhere and should only be included in the lab. When the lab is removed, these machines are not destroyed.
The path to your SSH private key used to connect to this VM. Only supported on PowerShell 6 and newer
The path to your SSH public key used to connect to this VM. Only supported on PowerShell 6 and newer.
The list of SUSE packages to install during AutoYast stage
The machine's time zone
The local tools path to be copied to the machine
The tools path on the destination machine
The list of Ubuntu packages to install during cloudinit stage
The locale to use
The virtualization host to use. It is recommended to define the default virtualization host when creating a new lab
Specify VM generation on Azure and Hyper-V. Only valid if supported, possible values are 1 and 2
Attempt to activate the Windows VM online or using KMS
The domain for the auto logon user
The password for the auto logon user
The user name for the auto logon user

The Azure properties. Don't add them unless you know what you are doing. Currently valid properties: 'ResourceGroupName', 'UseAllRoleSizes', 'RoleSize', 'LoadBalancerRdpPort', 'LoadBalancerWinRmHttpPort', 'LoadBalancerWinRmHttpsPort', 'SubnetName','UseByolImage'
The role size of the machine on Azure
The default domain for the machine
The disk names created by Add-LabDiskDefinition
The first DNS server for the machine
The second DNS server for the machine
The domain name of the machine.
Indicates that Windows firewall should be enabled on the machine
The default gateway for the machine
The HyperV properties. Currently valid properties: - AutomaticStartAction, default Nothing, refer to Set-VM
- AutomaticStartDelay, default 0, refer to Set-VM
- AutomaticStopAction, default ShutDown, refer to Set-VM
- EnableTpm, if value is 1, true or yes, TPM will be enabled
- EnableSecureBoot, if value is on, SecureBoot will be enabled
- SecureBootTemplate, MicrosoftWindows or MicrosoftUEFICertificateAuthority
Path to an initial mof (machine configuration) file. Applied at first boot.
Path to an initial meta.mof (LCM configuration) file. Applied at first boot.
The credentials of the installation user (i.e. the local admin)
The static private IP address of this machine
Indicates that the machine should be joined to a lab domain
If using AD-based KMS, specify the domain to look up the KMS key
If specifying a standalone KMS, specify its port
Name of a standalone KMS Server
Maximum dynamic memory for machine Specified in bytes and must be within range 128MB-128GB.
Startup memory for machine or, if -MinMemory or -MaxMemory is not specified, static memory for machine. Specified in bytes and must be within range 128MB-128GB.
Minimum dynamic memory for machine Specified in bytes and must be within range 128MB-128GB.
Name of machine Name must consist of characters a-z, A-Z, '-' or 0-9 and must be 1-15 in length.
The lab network to connect this machine to
The lab network adapter to connect this machine to
Notes to add to the machine
The lab operating system to use
The operating system version
The organizational unit to join this VM into
Indicates that the machine definition should be passed back to the caller
Post installation activities as defined with Get-LabPostInstallationActivity
List of activities to run before the machine is installed
Virtual processor count for machine
Path to a difference Reference Disk to be used for this machine
Name of the resource in the resource group
The RHEL packages to install
The machine's role definitions. See `[enum]::GetValues([AutomatedLab.Roles])` or <https://automatedlab.org/en/latest/Wiki/Roles/roles/> for more information.
Indicates that this machine is already deployed somewhere and should only be included in the lab. When the lab is removed, these machines are not destroyed.
The path to your SSH private key used to connect to this VM. Only supported on PowerShell 6 and newer
The path to your SSH public key used to connect to this VM. Only supported on PowerShell 6 and newer.
The list of SUSE packages to install during AutoYast stage
The machine's time zone
The local tools path to be copied to the machine
The tools path on the destination machine
The list of Ubuntu packages to install during cloudinit stage
The locale to use
The virtualization host to use. It is recommended to define the default virtualization host when creating a new lab
Specify VM generation on Azure and Hyper-V. Only valid if supported, possible values are 1 and 2

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 1 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: Add-MachineDefinition -Name Server1 -OperatingSystem 'Windows Server 2016 Technical Preview 5 SERVERDATACENTER' -ToolsPath 'C:\LabSources\MyLabTools1' -ToolsPathDestination 'C:\MyDistTools'

dev:remarks.maml:para: Adds a definition of a Hyper-V machine with the name 'Server1' with the operating system of 'Windows Server 2016 Technical Preview 5 SERVERDATACENTER'. Machine will not be domain joined but be placed in workgroup.

If using a folder for tools folder for each machine in the lab ie C:\MyLabTools1\Server1, C:\MyLabTools1\Server2 etc, you can use 'C:\MyLabTools1\<machine>' and AutomatedLab will map using the name of each machine to find the tools folder.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 2 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: Add-MachineDefinition -Name Server1 -OperatingSystem 'Windows Server 2008 R2 Datacenter (Full Installation)' -VirtualizationHost HyperV -Memory 1GB -MinMemory 512MB -MaxMemory 2GB -DomainName 'contoso.com'

dev:remarks.maml:para: Adds a definition of a Hyper-V machine with the name 'Server1' with the operating system of 'Windows Server 2008 R2 Datacenter (Full Installation)'.
Following parameters for the machine, will be auto-configured: - Processors
- IPv4 address and subnet mask
- DNS server
- Regional settings (will be set to match the host machine)
- Time zone (will be set to match the host machine)
- Userlocale (will be set to match the host machine)
- Windows firewall is disabled

- Hyper-V switch or Azure virtual network depending on -DefaultVirtualizationEngine parameter used for New-LabDefinition or -VirtualizationEngine parameter used for this function (Add-LabMachineDefinition)

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 3 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: Add-MachineDefinition -Name Server1 -OperatingSystem 'Windows Server 2012 R2 Datacenter (Server with a GUI)' -OperatingSystemVersion 6.3.9600.17415 -VirtualizationHost HyperV -Processors 2 -Memory 2GB -IpAddress '192.168.100.5/24' -Network Network1

dev:remarks.maml:para: Adds a definition of a Hyper-V machine with the name 'Server1' with the operating system of 'Windows Server 2012 R2 Datacenter (Server with a GUI)' with build version of '6.3.9600.17415'.
Memory will be set to 2GB (static).

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 4 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: Add-MachineDefinition -Name Server1 -OperatingSystem 'Windows Server 2012 R2 Datacenter (Server with a GUI)' -VirtualizationHost HyperV -DefaultDomain

dev:remarks.maml:para: Adds a definition of a Hyper-V machine with the name 'Server1' with the operating system of 'Windows Server 2012 R2 Datacenter (Server with a GUI)'.
Memory will be set to 2GB (static).

maml:description.maml:para: Adds a definition of a virtual network

For Hyper-V, a definition of a virtual switch is created associated with the address space for the virtual switch. For Azure, a definition of a virtual network is created associated with the address space for the virtual network.
Name of virtual network
Address space of virtual network defined in the format '192.168.10.0/24'
Virtualization engine for network
Extra properties for Hyper-V based virtual network. Options are; type of virtual switch and name of physical adapter to connect to (if type of virtual switch is 'External')

Extra properties for Azure based virtual network. Options are: SubnetName: The name of the subnet to create in the virtual network. AL does not segment virtual networks into subnets. One virtual network per network definition will be created SubnetAddressPrefix: The address prefix (e.g. 24) of the subnet that will be created. LocationName: The Azure location name (e.g. westeurope). Bear in mind that this should not differ from your lab's default location. DnsServers: Comma-separated DNS servers for the network. ConnectToVnets: Connections to other Lab VNETs by leveraging VNET peering. When connecting two or more lab networks through this parameter, please also specifiy this for all additional network definitions PeeringVnetResourceIds: Connections to VNETs not managed by AutomatedLab. A list of resource IDs for the peering is required in the form /subscriptions/<GUID>/resourceGroups/<RGNAME>/providers/Microsoft.Network/virtualNetworks/<VNETNAME>. All peerings will be created with VNET access, no GW transit, no forwarding and no remote gateways.
Which management adapter to use. Will add an IP address for that adapter.
Name of the resource in the resource group
Wheter or not the virtual network will be returned as an object
Indicates that NAT should be used - only valid on HyperV
Address space of virtual network defined in the format '192.168.10.0/24'

Extra properties for Azure based virtual network. Options are: SubnetName: The name of the subnet to create in the virtual network. AL does not segment virtual networks into subnets. One virtual network per network definition will be created SubnetAddressPrefix: The address prefix (e.g. 24) of the subnet that will be created. LocationName: The Azure location name (e.g. westeurope). Bear in mind that this should not differ from your lab's default location. DnsServers: Comma-separated DNS servers for the network. ConnectToVnets: Connections to other Lab VNETs by leveraging VNET peering. When connecting two or more lab networks through this parameter, please also specifiy this for all additional network definitions PeeringVnetResourceIds: Connections to VNETs not managed by AutomatedLab. A list of resource IDs for the peering is required in the form /subscriptions/<GUID>/resourceGroups/<RGNAME>/providers/Microsoft.Network/virtualNetworks/<VNETNAME>. All peerings will be created with VNET access, no GW transit, no forwarding and no remote gateways.
Extra properties for Hyper-V based virtual network. Options are; type of virtual switch and name of physical adapter to connect to (if type of virtual switch is 'External')
Which management adapter to use. Will add an IP address for that adapter.
Name of virtual network
Wheter or not the virtual network will be returned as an object
Name of the resource in the resource group
Indicates that NAT should be used - only valid on HyperV
Virtualization engine for network

dev:remarks.maml:para: Adds a definition of a virtual network with the name 'Network1' and with an address space of 192.168.10.0/24 (which is a subnet mask of 255.255.255.0). Hyper-V machines can then be configured (defined) to use this network using -Network parameter. Virtual switch type will be internal.

Adds a definition of a virtual network with the name 'Network2' but without an address space. When starting deployment, the address space will be determined by automatically scanning for an available address space. Hyper-V machines can then be configured (defined) to use this network using -Network parameter. Virtual switch type will be internal.

Adds a definition of a virtual network with the name 'Network3' and with an address space of 192.168.0.0/16 (which is a subnet mask of 255.255.0.0). Azure machines can then be configured (defined) to use this network using -Network parameter.

Adds a definition of a virtual network with the name 'Network4' and with an address space of 192.168.0.0/16 (which is a subnet mask of 255.255.0.0). A subnet will be created inside the virtual network called 'Subnet1'. Virtual network will be placed in Azure data center 'West Europe'. DNS server for virtual network will be '192.168.10.4' and a VPN gateway will be created and configured to route between the network created ('Network4') and 'Network8'' Azure machines can then be configured (defined) to use this network using -Network parameter.

Adds a definition of a virtual network with the name 'Network5' and with an address space of 192.168.10.0/24 (which is a subnet mask of 255.255.255.0). Type of Hyper-V virtual switch will be external and connected/bridged to the physical adapter with the name 'Ethernet adapter vEthernet (External)'. Machines can then be configured (defined) to use this network using -Network parameter.

maml:description.maml:para: Export lab as XML
Exports the whole lab definition as XML files in the standard path $env:ProgramData\AutomatedLab\Labs
Export the unattend.xml for all machines as well
Overwrite existing XML files
Do not display any output
Export the unattend.xml for all machines as well
Overwrite existing XML files
Do not display any output

dev:remarks.maml:para: Export the current lab definition (Get-LabDefinition, Get-Lab) to $env:ProgramData\AutomatedLab\Labs, overwriting any existing files

maml:description.maml:para: Measures the disk speed of the specified logical drive letter
Measures the disk speed of the specified logical drive letter. This is used by AutomatedLab when determining optimal placement of harddisk files of Hyper-V virtual machines.
This requires the tool WinSAT which is part of the module deployment. As such, this cmdlet only works on Windows.
Drive letter to measure
Number of measurements to perform
Drive letter to measure
Number of measurements to perform

dev:remarks.maml:para: Measure disk speed of drive C
Measure disk speed of drive D using 5 iterations (repetetive measurements) and return average measurement

maml:description.maml:para: Get available address space

Gets the available address space for the lab by examining the config setting AutomatedLab.DefaultAddressSpace (Get-PSFConfig -FullName AutomatedLab.DefaultAddressSpace) and then incrementing the network until a suitable network address space for the lab has been found.

dev:remarks.maml:para: On a fresh Hyper-V without any deployed virtual switches, should return 192.168.10.0/24, or the value of Get-PSFConfig -FullName AutomatedLab.DefaultAddressSpace

maml:description.maml:para: {{ Fill in the Description }}
{{ Fill Name Description }}
{{ Fill Name Description }}

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 1 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> {{ Add example code here }}

{{ Fill Name Description }}
{{ Fill Name Description }}

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 1 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> {{ Add example code here }}

dev:remarks.maml:para: {{ Add example description here }}

maml:description.maml:para: Gets the lab definition
Retrieves all lab definition information

dev:remarks.maml:para: Retrieves all lab definition information

maml:description.maml:para: Returns all definitions of Active Directory domains/forest in the lab
Returns all definitions of Active Directory domains/forest in the lab

dev:remarks.maml:para: Return the current domain definition that has been added to a lab.

maml:description.maml:para: Get pre/post-installation activity
Returns a new pre/post-installation activity that can be attached to machines
Instead of a regular script, add this as a custom role. Custom roles adhere to a specific format. For more information, just have a look at $labsources\CustomRoles
A folder of dependency files if necessary
Indicates that CredSSP should not be used for connections. Useful in existing environments that you connected to with -SkipDeployment
A list of functions that will be used inside your Installation Activity
Indicates that the target folder should be kept on the machine
The script file path of the script that is executed
A list of variables that are used in your Installation Activity
Instead of a regular script, add this as a custom role. Custom roles adhere to a specific format. For more information, just have a look at $labsources\CustomRoles
A folder of dependency files if necessary
Indicates that CredSSP should not be used for connections. Useful in existing environments that you connected to with -SkipDeployment
A list of functions that will be used inside your Installation Activity
Indicates that the target folder should be kept on the machine
The remote script file name
A list of variables that are used in your Installation Activity
Instead of a regular script, add this as a custom role. Custom roles adhere to a specific format. For more information, just have a look at $labsources\CustomRoles
Indicates that CredSSP should not be used for connections. Useful in existing environments that you connected to with -SkipDeployment
A list of functions that will be used inside your Installation Activity
The ISO image that should be mounted during post-installation
The script file path of the script that is executed
A list of variables that are used in your Installation Activity
Instead of a regular script, add this as a custom role. Custom roles adhere to a specific format. For more information, just have a look at $labsources\CustomRoles
Indicates that CredSSP should not be used for connections. Useful in existing environments that you connected to with -SkipDeployment
A list of functions that will be used inside your Installation Activity
The ISO image that should be mounted during post-installation
The remote script file name
A list of variables that are used in your Installation Activity
Instead of a regular script, add this as a custom role. Custom roles adhere to a specific format. For more information, just have a look at $labsources\CustomRoles
Indicates that CredSSP should not be used for connections. Useful in existing environments that you connected to with -SkipDeployment
A list of functions that will be used inside your Installation Activity
Indicates that the target folder should be kept on the machine
The hashtable of properties for your custom role, if a custom role was selected. Bear in mind that this is a Dictionary<string,string>, and requires your input to consist of strings only.
A list of variables that are used in your Installation Activity
Instead of a regular script, add this as a custom role. Custom roles adhere to a specific format. For more information, just have a look at $labsources\CustomRoles
A folder of dependency files if necessary
Indicates that CredSSP should not be used for connections. Useful in existing environments that you connected to with -SkipDeployment
A list of functions that will be used inside your Installation Activity
The ISO image that should be mounted during post-installation
Indicates that the target folder should be kept on the machine
The hashtable of properties for your custom role, if a custom role was selected. Bear in mind that this is a Dictionary<string,string>, and requires your input to consist of strings only.
The remote script file name
The script file path of the script that is executed
A list of variables that are used in your Installation Activity

dev:remarks.maml:para: Configures the custom role ProGet5 with two role properties.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 2 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: $postInstallActivity = @() $postInstallActivity += Get-LabPostInstallationActivity -ScriptFileName 'New-ADLabAccounts 2.0.ps1' -DependencyFolder $labSources\PostInstallationActivities\PrepareFirstChildDomain $postInstallActivity += Get-LabPostInstallationActivity -ScriptFileName PrepareRootDomain.ps1 -DependencyFolder $labSources\PostInstallationActivities\PrepareRootDomain Add-LabMachineDefinition -Name POSHDC1 -Memory 512MB -Roles RootDC -IpAddress 192.168.30.10 -PostInstallationActivity $postInstallActivity

dev:remarks.maml:para: Create objects in your lab domain: 5995 users, 138 OUs, 138 groups, sites and site links and more.

maml:description.maml:para: Returns all ISO definitions in the lab
Returns all image definitions currently found in the lab

dev:remarks.maml:para: Returns a list of ISOs that have been added with Add-LabIsoImageDefinition

maml:description.maml:para: Returns all machine definitions in the lab
Returns all machine definitions in the lab
Indicates that all definitions should be returned
The machine definitions to return
The roles to filter the machine definitions on. See `[enum]::GetValues([AutomatedLab.Roles])` or <https://automatedlab.org/en/latest/Wiki/Roles/roles/> for more information.
Indicates that all definitions should be returned
The machine definitions to return
The roles to filter the machine definitions on. See `[enum]::GetValues([AutomatedLab.Roles])` or <https://automatedlab.org/en/latest/Wiki/Roles/roles/> for more information.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Get all WebServer machine definitions

maml:description.maml:para: Get a role definition
Gets a role definition to be used with the parameter Roles for a new virtual machine definition
Indicates that all available roles should be returned.
The role names. See `[enum]::GetValues([AutomatedLab.Roles])` or <https://automatedlab.org/en/latest/Wiki/Roles/roles/> for more information.
The properties for one role definition that can be set for the role, e.g. $role = Get-LabMachineRoleDefinition -Role FirstChildDC -Properties @{ ParentDomain = 'contoso.com'; NewDomain = 'child' }
Indicates that Property-Syntax should be returned
Indicates that all available roles should be returned.
The properties for one role definition that can be set for the role, e.g. $role = Get-LabMachineRoleDefinition -Role FirstChildDC -Properties @{ ParentDomain = 'contoso.com'; NewDomain = 'child' }
The role names. See `[enum]::GetValues([AutomatedLab.Roles])` or <https://automatedlab.org/en/latest/Wiki/Roles/roles/> for more information.
Indicates that Property-Syntax should be returned

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 1 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: $role = Get-LabMachineRoleDefinition -Role FirstChildDC -Properties @{ ParentDomain = 'contoso.com'; NewDomain = 'child' } Add-LabMachineDefinition -Name Host -Role $role

dev:remarks.maml:para: Gets a role definition for the first DC of a child domain with the additional properties ParentDomain = contoso.com and the child domain name NewDomain = child
Returns all possible parameter for the specified role

maml:description.maml:para: Returns all existing virtual networks (switches) on a Hyper-V host
Returns all existing virtual networks (switches) within the imported lab. If the `-All` switch is used, all virtual networks (switches) on the Hyper-V host are returned.
The name of the virtual network.
Indicates that all switches on the Hyper-V host should be returned.
The name of the virtual network.
Indicates that all switches on the Hyper-V host should be returned.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 1 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> Get-LabVirtualNetwork | Format-Table Name, AddressSpace

dev:remarks.maml:para: Get the network name and address space of all lab networks, e.g. Name AddressSpace ---- ------------ DscWorkshop 192.168.111.0/24 Default Switch 172.18.29.64/28

maml:description.maml:para: Returns all virtual network definitions in the lab
Returns all virtual network definitions in the lab
The address space of the network in CIDR notation
The name of the network
The address space of the network in CIDR notation
The name of the network

dev:remarks.maml:para: Tries to locate the network definition that has the address space 192.168.2.0/24

maml:description.maml:para: Get lab volumes
Get all file-backed virtual volumes on all disks with drive letter and serial number

dev:remarks.maml:para: Return all physical disks on the lab host

maml:description.maml:para: Import an existing lab definition to extend it later on.
Import an existing lab definition to extend it later on.
Name of the exported lab to import.
Indicates that the imported data should be returned
Path to the lab definition files.
Indicates that the imported data should be returned
Name of the exported lab to import.
Indicates that the imported data should be returned
Path to the lab definition files.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Imports the lab named POSH and stores the definition in a variable.

maml:description.maml:para: Creates a new lab definition
Needed for defining a lab definition which is a mandatory 'container' for the lab to be deployed. To be called before any other function of AutomatedLab.
Name of lab. Name must only contain character a-z, A-Z and 0-9.
Path of placement of Hyper-V based machines
Specifies the size of the base disk to create for each used Operating System
Maximum memory to use for lab if using automatic configuration of memory for each machine (by not specifying the memory at all)
Notes to add to the lab
Virtualization engine to use as default for the lab. When not specifying the -VirtualizationHost parameter
Indicates that the created definition should also be returned

Instructs AutomatedLab to use 80% of all available memory for the lab where the machines will split this memory in a weighted approach based on what role(s) the machines are to have. This is only true if the memory is NOT specified when defining machine using Add-LabMachineDefinition.
Instructs AutomatedLab to only configure static memory for all machines if using automatic configuration of memory for each machine (by not specifying the memory at all).
Virtualization engine to use as default for the lab. When not specifying the -VirtualizationHost parameter
Maximum memory to use for lab if using automatic configuration of memory for each machine (by not specifying the memory at all)
Name of lab. Name must only contain character a-z, A-Z and 0-9.
Notes to add to the lab
Indicates that the created definition should also be returned
Specifies the size of the base disk to create for each used Operating System

Instructs AutomatedLab to use 80% of all available memory for the lab where the machines will split this memory in a weighted approach based on what role(s) the machines are to have. This is only true if the memory is NOT specified when defining machine using Add-LabMachineDefinition.
Instructs AutomatedLab to only configure static memory for all machines if using automatic configuration of memory for each machine (by not specifying the memory at all).
Path of placement of Hyper-V based machines

dev:remarks.maml:para: Creates a new lab definition with the name MyTestLab1

Creates a new lab definition with the name MyTestLab1 and instructs AutomatedLab that all machines being added to the lab, is to be Hyper-V based machines unless anything else is specified using the -VirtualizationHost parameter on Add-LabMachineDefinition.

Creates a new lab definition with the name MyTestLab1 and instructs AutomatedLab that all machines being added to the lab, is to be Azure based machines unless anything else is specified using the -VirtualizationHost parameter on Add-LabMachineDefinition.

Creates a new lab definition with the name MyTestLab1 and instructs AutomatedLab that all machines being added to the lab, is to be Hyper-V based machines unless anything else is specified using the -VirtualizationHost parameter on Add-LabMachineDefinition.

Creates a new lab definition with the name MyTestLab1 and instructs AutomatedLab that all machines being added to the lab, is to be Hyper-V based machines unless anything else is specified using the -VirtualizationHost parameter on Add-LabMachineDefinition.

If 8GB of memory if more than 80% of the available memory, the memory will be limited to 8GB for the entire lab where the machines will split these 8GB in a weighted approach based on what role(s) the machines are to have. This is only true if the memory is NOT specified when defining machine using Add-LabMachineDefinition.

Creates a new lab definition with the name MyTestLab1 and instructs AutomatedLab that all machines being added to the lab, is to be Hyper-V based machines unless anything else is specified using the -VirtualizationHost parameter on Add-LabMachineDefinition.

80% of all available memory will be used for the entire lab where the machines will split this memory in a weighted approach based on what role(s) the machines are to have. This is only true if the memory is NOT specified when defining machine using Add-LabMachineDefinition.

Creates a new lab definition with the name MyTestLab1 and instructs AutomatedLab that all machines being added to the lab, is to be Hyper-V based machines unless anything else is specified using the -VirtualizationHost parameter on Add-LabMachineDefinition.
All machines will be using static memory as opposed the default dynamic memory.

maml:description.maml:para: Creates a network adapter definition roughly interpreted as a NIC
Creates a network adapter definition roughly interpreted as a NIC. This NIC can then be connected to a lab machine when defining the machine using Add-LabMachineDefinition
The VLAN ID to configure
List of DNS suffixes to append
Indicates that parent suffixes should be appended to the DNS suffix
The DNS suffix (Windows)
Indicates that this adapter will be the default adapter
Indicates that the DNS suffix should be included in the DNS registration
The name of the interface on the VM
The IP address of the adapter
The DNS servers to use
The gateway that should be used
The IPv6 address of this adapter
The IPv6 prefix
The IPv6 DNS server list
The IPv6 gateway to configure
The hardware address of the virtual adapter
Indicates that this adapter is used as a management adapter
The NetBIOS options to set (Default, Enabled, Disabled)
Indicates that the name should be registered in DNS
The name of the virtual switch.
The VLAN ID to configure
List of DNS suffixes to append
Indicates that parent suffixes should be appended to the DNS suffix
The DNS suffix (Windows)
Indicates that this adapter will be the default adapter
Indicates that the DNS suffix should be included in the DNS registration
The name of the interface on the VM
The DNS servers to use
The IPv6 DNS server list
The hardware address of the virtual adapter
Indicates that this adapter is used as a management adapter
The NetBIOS options to set (Default, Enabled, Disabled)
Indicates that the name should be registered in DNS
Indicates that DHCP should be used. Useful e.g. for the Default Switch
The name of the virtual switch.
The VLAN ID to configure
List of DNS suffixes to append
Indicates that parent suffixes should be appended to the DNS suffix
The DNS suffix (Windows)
Indicates that this adapter will be the default adapter
Indicates that the DNS suffix should be included in the DNS registration
The name of the interface on the VM
The IP address of the adapter
The DNS servers to use
The gateway that should be used
The IPv6 address of this adapter
The IPv6 prefix
The IPv6 DNS server list
The IPv6 gateway to configure
The hardware address of the virtual adapter
Indicates that this adapter is used as a management adapter
The NetBIOS options to set (Default, Enabled, Disabled)
Indicates that the name should be registered in DNS
Indicates that DHCP should be used. Useful e.g. for the Default Switch
The name of the virtual switch.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 1 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: $netAdapter = @() $netAdapter += New-LabNetworkAdapterDefinition -VirtualSwitch $labName -Ipv4Address 192.168.30.50 $netAdapter += New-LabNetworkAdapterDefinition -VirtualSwitch 'Default Switch' -UseDhcp Add-LabMachineDefinition -Name POSHFS1 -Memory 512MB -Roles FileServer, Routing -NetworkAdapter $netAdapter

dev:remarks.maml:para: In order to create a machine capable of routing, you can assign two network adapter definitions to it

maml:description.maml:para: Remove a domain definition
Removes a domain definition from the lab domain definitions
The name of the domain definition to remove
The name of the domain definition to remove

dev:remarks.maml:para: In order not to start over entirely, remove a single domain definition from the lab

maml:description.maml:para: Remove ISO definition
Removes an ISO image from the lab's image definitions
The ISO name
The ISO name

dev:remarks.maml:para: Remove the ISO for SQL Server 2017 from the current lab definition

maml:description.maml:para: Remove a machine definition
Removes a machine definition from the current lab
The machine name
The machine name

dev:remarks.maml:para: Remove DC02 from the lab machine definitions

maml:description.maml:para: Remove a virtual network definition
Removes a virtual network definition from the lab's network definitions
The name of the virtual network
The name of the virtual network

dev:remarks.maml:para: Remove the network definition Lab01

maml:description.maml:para: Repair duplicate IPs
Modifies duplicate IP addresses for machines by incrementing the address by one

dev:remarks.maml:para: Repair any duplicate IP addresses

maml:description.maml:para: Helper cmdlet to update the lab definition
Helper cmdlet to update the lab definition
The lab definition
A list of machine definitions
A list of disk definitions
A list of disk definitions
The lab definition
A list of machine definitions

dev:remarks.maml:para: Update the existing lab definition with a current list of machines and disks

maml:description.maml:para: Set the VM disk container
Automatically determines the disk to store all VMs on by latency. Boot volumes are only selected if no other volumes are present or if the next drive is more than 50% slower than the boot drive.
The space needed for the whole lab installation
The space needed for the whole lab installation

dev:remarks.maml:para: Locates a disk with at least 20GB free space to place the VMs on.

maml:description.maml:para: Validates the lab definition

Use this function to (manually) validate the lab definition before attempting to actually deploy it. A lot of checks is being done which can potentially avoid issues up front instead of having to wait for these to occur during deployment and perhaps after considerably long time.
The path to an exported lab definition
Indicates that no console messages should be displayed
The path to an exported lab definition
Indicates that no console messages should be displayed

dev:remarks.maml:para: Performs validation of the lab saved in the file 'D:\MyLabs\MyTestLab\Lab.xml'

Type Message TargetObject ---- ------- ------------ Information Role defined RootDC Information Role defined FirstChildDC Information Role defined DC Information Machine defined in lab DC1 Information Machine defined in lab DC2 Information Machine defined in lab DC3 Information Machine defined in lab SRV1 Information Domain defined contoso.com Information Domain defined child.contoso.com Summary Ok Lab True
Performs validation of the lab saved in the file 'D:\MyLabs\MyTestLab\Lab.xml'.
